title: Email Welcome Sequence
description: Design and write a 5-email welcome sequence that takes a new subscriber from signup to first purchase, building trust and delivering value at each step.
whyItMatters: Welcome emails get 4x higher open rates and 5x higher click rates than regular emails. The first few emails a subscriber receives set the tone for the entire relationship. A strong welcome sequence can drive significant revenue on autopilot.
prerequisites: Familiarity with email as a channel

steps.title: Define the scenario
steps.description: Choose a brand and a signup trigger. Example: someone subscribes to a newsletter, creates an account, or downloads a freebie. Define what action you want them to take by email 5 (usually: first purchase).

steps.title: Map the sequence journey
steps.description: Plan the emotional and informational arc across 5 emails: Welcome → Value → Trust → Desire → Action. Each email has one clear purpose.
steps.tips: Think of the sequence as a story, not 5 random emails.

steps.title: Write Email 1: Welcome
steps.description: Delivered immediately after signup. Thank them, set expectations, deliver any promised content (lead magnet), and introduce the brand personality. Subject line is critical.

steps.title: Write Email 2: Value
steps.description: Delivered 2 days later. Share your best piece of content — a tip, guide, or insight. Demonstrate expertise without selling anything.

steps.title: Write Email 3: Trust
steps.description: Delivered 4 days later. Share social proof — customer stories, testimonials, or results. Build credibility.

steps.title: Write Email 4: Desire
steps.description: Delivered 6 days later. Highlight a specific product/service benefit. Address common objections. Show the transformation.

steps.title: Write Email 5: Action
steps.description: Delivered 8 days later. Clear CTA with an incentive — discount, free trial, or limited offer. Create urgency without being pushy.

steps.title: Optimise details
steps.description: Write subject lines (2-3 options per email for A/B testing), preview text, and CTAs. Define timing and segmentation rules.

deliverables: 5 complete emails with subject lines and preview text
Sequence flow diagram showing timing and triggers
A/B test plan for subject lines
Brief strategy document explaining the approach

rubric.description: Each email builds on the last with a clear progression toward the goal.
Subject lines are compelling, body copy is scannable, and CTAs are clear.
All 5 emails feel like they come from the same brand with a consistent tone.
The sequence delivers genuine value before asking for anything.
